title: Competitor Analysis
grain: one row per outsourced service/process line served by a third-party service provider at a named group (company), with process-area flags and an account-pursuit status
summary: Each row records a group (company) and a third-party service provider engaged for a specific process line, tagged with which functional areas the engagement covers (Claims, UW, Premium Audit, F&A, Platform), an approximate FTE count, and a free-text status/comment on the pursuit. It is an account-tracking sheet mapping which competitor/service provider holds which work at each group, plus contract-renewal timing and an internal owner. It lets you see who is servicing a group, in what process areas, at what scale, and where things stand.
use_cases: Which service providers are engaged at a given group (e.g. Liberty Mutual or Travelers), and in what process areas?
Which groups does a particular service provider (e.g. CTS, Genpact, Insurity) serve?
Which engagements cover Claims vs Underwriting (UW) vs F&A vs Premium Audit vs Platform?
Which accounts have a contract renewal coming up (e.g. < 6 Months)?
How many FTEs are deployed on a given engagement or across a group?
Which accounts are flagged Strategic vs Named, and who is the internal owner?

columns.name: S No
columns.note: row serial number

columns.name: GROUP NAME
columns.note: group/company being tracked; repeats across rows (one row per process line)

columns.name: Type
columns.note: ownership type: Mutual / Public / Private/Mutual; typically set only on a group's first row

columns.name: Strategic / Named
columns.note: account tier: Named / Named Backup / Strategic

columns.name: EXL Contact
columns.note: column present but entirely empty

columns.name: Service Provider
columns.note: third-party provider serving the process line (e.g. CTS, Genpact, Insurity, Medata, NTT)

columns.name: Contract Renewal
columns.note: renewal window when noted: < 6 Months / 1 Year

columns.name: Claims
columns.note: Y flags the engagement covers Claims

columns.name: UW
columns.note: Y flags Underwriting coverage

columns.name: Premium Audit
columns.note: Y flags Premium Audit coverage (only Y observed)

columns.name: F&A
columns.note: Y flags Finance & Accounting coverage (only Y observed)

columns.name: Platform
columns.note: Y flags Platform coverage (only Y observed)

columns.name: FTEs
columns.note: approximate headcount on the engagement

columns.name: Comments (Processes etc.)
columns.note: free-text description of processes covered

columns.name: Current Status - 2/21
columns.note: free-text pursuit status; header dates the snapshot to 2/21 (Feb 2021)

columns.name: Owner
columns.note: internal owner (first name or full name)

columns.name: col16
columns.note: unnamed, entirely empty column

columns.name: Existing EXL Client
columns.note: column present but entirely empty

columns.name: col18
columns.note: unnamed, entirely empty column

columns.name: < 6 Months
columns.note: off-to-the-side stray column (values '2 Year','3 Year'); appears to be a legend/secondary-table fragment, not part of the main grain

examples.Comments (Processes etc.): Personal Insurance, Commercial Insurnace - From new business to claims settlement
examples.Current Status - 2/21: Champion Challenger concept in discussion. Rohit has met Mojgan. Subsequent follow-ups in progress. Sharing capability material as next step

not_answerable.q: Who is the EXL contact for a given account?
not_answerable.reason: the 'EXL Contact' column is present but 100% empty

not_answerable.q: Is a given group an existing EXL client?
not_answerable.reason: the 'Existing EXL Client' column is present but 100% empty

not_answerable.q: What is the contract value or revenue of an engagement?
not_answerable.reason: no monetary/contract-value column exists (only FTE counts)

not_answerable.q: How has an engagement's status changed over time?
not_answerable.reason: this is a single snapshot (dated 2/21); no time series is present

warnings: vintage inferred from column header 'Current Status - 2/21' (Feb 2021); confirm as-of with data owner
stray column '< 6 Months' with values '2 Year'/'3 Year' appears to be a legend/secondary-table fragment off to the side, not part of the main grain
columns 'EXL Contact', 'Existing EXL Client', 'col16', 'col18' are entirely empty
'Type' and 'Strategic / Named' are sparse (typically populated only on a group's first row)
